rag_rules: Place assumptions in component type annexes unless they require implementation-level bindings, then use implementation annexes.
Ensure referenced identifiers in AGREE clauses are visible in the target scope via AADL-extracted ports, constants, or variables.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees must use only scalar numeric or boolean expressions derived from visible AADL elements.
Avoid encoding AADL properties directly; rely on explicitly exposed typed variables or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes use the format: annex agree {** ... **};
Range comparisons should be expressed as boolean expressions: x >= min and x <= max.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Do not convert Boolean or opaque data ports into numeric range comparisons.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
